assertion_results.text: The verification report marks all 5 acceptance criteria as PASS — the diff implements single license filter, comma-separated filter, 400 validation, pagination integration, and unchanged response shape
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 12: 'Acceptance Criteria | PASS | 5 of 5 criteria met'. Lines 57-65 detail each criterion individually, all marked PASS: (1) single license filter, (2) comma-separated filter, (3) 400 Bad Request validation, (4) pagination integration, (5) unchanged response shape. Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md each have 'Verdict: PASS' confirming all 5 acceptance criteria pass.

assertion_results.text: Scope containment is PASS — all files in the diff (list.rs, service/mod.rs, tests/api/package.rs) are listed in the task's Files to Modify or Files to Create
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 7: 'Scope Containment | PASS | PR modifies exactly the 3 files specified in the task (2 modified, 1 created); no out-of-scope or unimplemented files'. Lines 22-25 provide file-by-file detail: 'Files to Modify: modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs (modified), modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs (modified)' and 'Files to Create: tests/api/package.rs (created)'. Line 25: 'No out-of-scope files. No unimplemented files.'

assertion_results.text: No sub-tasks are created because there are no review comments (Review Feedback is N/A)
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 5: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments or review body items on this PR'. Line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'. No mention of sub-task creation anywhere in any output file.

assertion_results.text: The skill does NOT modify any code files — it only reads, verifies, and reports (constraint 1.11)
assertion_results.evidence: The outputs directory contains only report files: report.md and criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md. The report at line 112 ends with a generated comment signature. All output files are analysis/verification documents. There is no evidence in any output file that code files were modified — the report consistently uses language like 'The PR introduces', 'The handler's return type remains unchanged', showing read-only analysis.

assertion_results.text: The report does NOT auto-merge the PR — it presents findings for human review (constraint 1.13)
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 107: 'Overall: PASS' followed by a summary of findings at lines 109-110. The report ends with an AI-generated comment signature at line 112. There is no merge action, no 'gh pr merge' command, and no mention of auto-merging. The report presents findings as a verification report for human consumption.

assertion_results.text: Sensitive pattern scan is PASS — the diff contains no passwords, API keys, or private keys
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 10: 'Sensitive Patterns | PASS | No secrets, credentials, API keys, or sensitive patterns detected in added lines across all 3 files'. Lines 39-47 provide detailed scan results listing specific pattern categories checked (hardcoded passwords, API keys/tokens, private keys, environment files with secrets, cloud credentials, database credentials) — all negative. Line 47: 'The diff contains only Rust source code (endpoint handler logic, service layer query building, and integration tests) with no sensitive patterns.'

assertion_results.text: The report contains a Test Change Classification row with ADDITIVE — only new test files were added (tests/api/package.rs is a new file)
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 14: 'Test Change Classification | ADDITIVE | All test changes are in the newly created file tests/api/package.rs; 4 new test functions, 0 removed'. Lines 99-105 provide details: 'tests/api/package.rs is a newly created file containing 4 new test functions. No test files were modified or deleted. All test changes are purely additive: +4 test functions, +0 removed test functions, +0 assertions removed or relaxed, +0 skip annotations added'.

assertion_results.text: The verification report assembles verdicts from all four domain sub-agents: Scope Containment, Diff Size, and Commit Traceability from Intent Alignment; Sensitive Patterns from Security; CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, and Verification Commands from Correctness; Test Quality (combining Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, and Eval Quality) and Test Change Classification from Style/Conventions
assertion_results.evidence: The report.md summary table (lines 5-15) contains all required verdict rows: Review Feedback, Root-Cause Investigation, Scope Containment, Diff Size, Commit Traceability, Sensitive Patterns, CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, Test Quality (with sub-components: 'Repetitive Test Detection: PASS; Test Documentation: PASS; Eval Quality: N/A'), Test Change Classification, and Verification Commands. These are organized under domain headers: 'Intent Alignment' (lines 17-29, containing Scope Containment, Diff Size, Commit Traceability), 'Security' (lines 31-47, containing Sensitive Pattern Scan), 'Correctness' (lines 49-69, containing CI Status, Acceptance Criteria, Verification Commands), and 'Style/Conventions' (lines 71-105, containing Convention Upgrade, Repetitive Test Detection, Test Documentation, Eval Quality, Test Change Classification).

assertion_results.text: The report includes detailed findings with specific evidence for each domain — file-by-file scope comparison (Intent Alignment), line-level pattern scanning results (Security), per-criterion code-level verification (Correctness), and test quality assessment (Style/Conventions) — not just pass/fail verdicts in the summary table
assertion_results.evidence: Intent Alignment (lines 19-29): file-by-file comparison listing exact files 'modules/fundamental/src/package/endpoints/list.rs (modified), modules/fundamental/src/package/service/mod.rs (modified)' and 'tests/api/package.rs (created)' plus diff size of '~110 lines'. Security (lines 37-47): lists 6 specific pattern categories scanned (hardcoded passwords, API keys/tokens, private keys, environment files, cloud credentials, database credentials). Correctness (lines 55-68): per-criterion code-level verification with function names (validate_license_param, is_in filter, Condition::any()), test names (test_list_packages_single_license_filter etc.), and specific assertion details. Style/Conventions (lines 77-105): individual test function analysis with names and distinct logic patterns, documentation comment text quoted verbatim, and detailed additive classification with counts (+4/+0/+0/+0). Additionally, criterion-1.md through criterion-5.md provide extensive code-level evidence with Rust code snippets for each acceptance criterion.

assertion_results.text: Eval Quality is N/A because no eval result reviews exist in the PR — no reviews match the eval result detection criteria, so Eval Quality does not affect the Test Quality combination
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md line 13: 'Test Quality | PASS | Repetitive Test Detection: PASS (4 tests with distinct logic, not parameterizable); Test Documentation: PASS (all 4 test functions have /// doc comments); Eval Quality: N/A'. Line 95-96: 'Eval Quality -- N/A' and 'No eval result reviews found on this PR.' The Test Quality verdict is PASS based only on Repetitive Test Detection and Test Documentation, with Eval Quality not affecting the combination.

assertion_results.text: No eval failure sub-tasks are created because Eval Quality is N/A — eval failure sub-tasks are only created when Eval Quality is WARN (at least one eval assertion failed)
assertion_results.evidence: Eval Quality is N/A (report.md line 95-96: 'Eval Quality -- N/A, No eval result reviews found on this PR.'). No mention of eval failure sub-task creation anywhere in any output file. The report shows no sub-tasks created at all (line 6: 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate').

assertion_results.text: Review Feedback and Root-Cause Investigation verdicts are determined by the orchestrator independently from domain analysis — Review Feedback is N/A because no review comments exist, Root-Cause Investigation is N/A because no sub-tasks were created
assertion_results.evidence: In report.md lines 5-6: 'Review Feedback | N/A | No review comments or review body items on this PR' and 'Root-Cause Investigation | N/A | No sub-tasks created; nothing to investigate'. These two verdicts appear at the top of the summary table (lines 5-6), before the domain-specific sections (Intent Alignment starts at line 17, Security at line 31, Correctness at line 49, Style/Conventions at line 71), indicating they are determined independently from the domain sub-agent analysis. Neither verdict appears under any domain heading.
